_readme: Sense-split-first construction DESIGN assumption block ASM-1900..1909 — EMITTED by the Fable DESIGN agent designer-4 (2026-07-12) for central registration by the coordinator with the commit; registry/assumptions.jsonl is NOT touched by this pass (no git actions).
Range 1900-1909 verified free against registry/assumptions.jsonl at emission time (max registered id 1897; repo-wide grep for ASM-19[0-9][0-9] empty); ids 1900-1909 used.

Scope: docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md — the kernel-v1 sense-split-first construction pipeline (sense-inventory import, per-sense minting/migration, per-sense NSM explication + UFO typing, scale-track integration, cheap first validation, staging). This is a DESIGN for maintainer approval, not an experiment commitment; it modifies no frozen record, no encoder pin, no mechanical verdict, and states no feasibility conclusion on CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY.

Rider carried by every forward-looking row, verbatim: DESIGN-ONLY, maintainer approval required before any build or spend; PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY where a proxy judge pair is involved; soft selectional typing stays rank-only non-binding, never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.

Tags: MEASURED = read from committed bytes or a stated local diagnostic this tick (arithmetic on such bytes folded in, steps shown in the design doc); LIT-BACKED = published-literature grounding, provenance disclosed; STIPULATED = design choice or this designer's ruling, rationale given, never evidence; EXTRAPOLATION = forward claim, resolution_path given, never a premise.

rows.claim: Polysemy census against the pinned WordNet 3.1 extraction (data/lexical-wn31/synsets-{noun,verb,adj,adv}.jsonl, lemma membership in annotations.lemmas, same-POS counting): 51 of the 54 kernel-v0 words are single-lemma matchable (part-of, has-part, maker-of excluded as multiword coinages); they cover 434 same-POS synsets in total (median 4 per word); 42/51 words have >=2 same-POS senses and 24 have >=5. Heavy tail: break 59 verb synsets (75 across all POS), make 49, give 44, take 42, dead 17, find 16. Of the 39 g2-panel concepts, 24 words have >=4 same-POS senses. This pins locally the adjudication's previously LIT-BACKED-unpinned '59 WordNet verb senses for break' (ASM-1885 resolution path partially discharged).
rows.rationale: Fixes the raw size of the sense inventory the construction must reconcile, the raw sense-multiplier ceiling (434/51 ~ 8.5x), and the Stage-B polysemous-panel-subset count (24 words).
rows.backing_ref: data/lexical-wn31/synsets-*.jsonl + manifest.json (source sha256:3f7d8be8...); data/kernel-v0/manifest.json (54 concepts); data/onto-softtype/soft-type-candidates.jsonl (39 panel concepts); counting script shown in design session (same-POS lemma membership)

rows.claim: Exactly 8 pinned FrameNet 1.7 frames carry a 'break' lexical unit (data/onto-framenet/frames.jsonl): Cause_to_fragment (frame-414, agentive physical), Breaking_apart (1862, inchoative physical), Breaking_off (1860), Render_nonfunctional (416), Cause_harm (112), Experience_bodily_harm (384), Compliance (117, 'Acts and State_of_Affairs for which Protagonists are responsible' — the break-a-promise/law norm-violation sense), Opportunity (2350, break.n). The adjudication's claim that FrameNet separates the physical Cause_to_fragment from the norm-violating Compliance is confirmed at source; frame boundaries partition break's coarse senses and every break frame is eventive (or, for the nominal, an event-adjacent schema).
rows.rationale: Grounds the frame-disjointness split criterion (R3) and the structural satisfaction of the maintainer's 'a breakage is an EVENT in every sense' clause; discharges the FrameNet half of ASM-1885's pinning path.
rows.backing_ref: data/onto-framenet/frames.jsonl (frames 1862,1860,112,414,117,384,2350,416 with lexicalUnits break.*); data/onto-framenet/manifest.json (FrameNet 1.7, CC BY 3.0, sha-pinned source)

rows.claim: Sense-inventory reconciliation rule for kernel-v1 construction: (R1) candidate senses = all same-POS WordNet 3.1 synsets of the lemma, enumerated mechanically; (R2) each candidate is projected onto the FrameNet frame carrying that lemma's LU where one matches, else frame:null; (R3) a kernel-v1 concept is a cluster of >=1 candidate synsets that share ONE FrameNet frame (or are all frame-null), one truthful NSM explication, and one argument structure (causative/inchoative never clustered) — the frame boundary is a HARD split criterion, within-frame clustering of WordNet's over-fine senses is permitted with member synsets listed; (R4) mint only kernel-needed senses, with every non-minted candidate recorded in an explicit excludedSenses list on the lemma's sense-index record (scope closed by construction, not prose); (R5) single-source candidates (FN LU without WN synset or vice versa) enter with disclosed single-source provenance, nothing silently dropped.
rows.rationale: The load-bearing design choice: reconciles WordNet's over-fineness (59 break verb synsets) with FrameNet's coarser 8-frame partition without either re-merging distinct senses (g2's defect) or forcing 59 explications per verb. R4's exclusion lists are what convert 'this concept means the word' into 'this concept means this synset cluster and not those'.
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 1.2-1.3 (worked break table); docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md section C (sense-split-first ordering ruling)

rows.claim: Minting rule: alias URN urn:kernel-v1:<lemma>.<senseTag> (senseTag a short stable per-lemma-unique mnemonic, e.g. break.shatter / break.violate / break.malfunction; annotation, outside identity); identity remains the content-addressed urn:kot: mint over the kot-ast/1 explication object via the pinned mint tool (mintToolHash e07bc2ac...), so kernel-v1 requires NO encoder change, NO schema change, NO ALGORITHM_VERSION bump and NO X0 golden regeneration. Each concept carries an annotation sense record {senseTag, wn31Synsets, framenetFrame, glossQuote}; each lemma a sense-index record with excludedSenses. Fail-closed corpus gates: G1 duplicateIdentityGroups must stay 0 — two senses receiving byte-identical explications are merged and the merge REPORTED as a measured profile-1 expressivity limit, never silently absorbed; G2 every lemma with >=2 same-POS WN senses must have >=2 minted senses or 1 minted sense + non-empty excludedSenses, else the build fails with ERR_SENSE_UNDERDETERMINED.
rows.rationale: Keeps sense-splitting entirely off the encoder-version critical path (data-corpus change only, per the repo convention that the content-hash pin governs {schema, algorithm, D, codebook, weighting} — none touched) while making unacknowledged polysemy a build error rather than a judge-discovered defect.
rows.backing_ref: data/kernel-v0/manifest.json (minting block: identityPayload, mintToolHash, duplicateIdentityGroups); CLAUDE.md encoder-version convention; docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 2.1

rows.claim: Migration policy: kernel-v0 stays frozen forever (frozen experiments reference it); kernel-v1 is a new corpus at data/kernel-v1/ with a TOTAL per-concept disposition map recorded via supersedes/supersededBy — CARRY (already sense-fixed concepts, e.g. 'lie (the words)': explication byte-identical, hence the SAME content-addressed urn:kot: mint, continuity for free; sense record seeded from the existing hand alignment rows), SEED (polysemous word-level concepts whose explication describes one sense: deprecated, explication becomes that sense's draft — v0 break -> break.shatter, per its own authoring note), REVIEW (latent flagged cases: friend 040/041; broken, whose downstream repair reference semantically wants break.malfunction not break.shatter, demonstrating the reference DAG is sense-sensitive and must be re-pointed edge by edge). WN/FN alignment files, soft-type records and molecules-v0 references are re-emitted against v1; a repo grep gate in the Stage-C build fails on any consumer still pointing at a deprecated word-level URN.
rows.rationale: Preserves reproducibility of every frozen lineage while making the deprecation total — a dangling word-level reference would silently re-import the exact scope bug the split exists to remove.
rows.backing_ref: data/lexical-wn31/alignment-kernel-v0.json (existing per-concept hand sense picks); data/kernel-v0/manifest.json (references DAG: broken->break, repair->broken); docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 2.2

rows.claim: Per-sense explication + typing scheme: the authoring brief (WN glosses, FN frame definition, Core FEs, examples) is auto-assembled and the valency skeleton auto-suggested, but the NSM explication body is ALWAYS authored through the encoder/validator loop (auto paraphrase never promotes to Explicated, per scale-track section 3.4 rule 7). Typing is two-level and per-sense: (a) concept-level CK-UFO ontic category assigned via the deterministic cascade — all break.* senses are events (the maintainer clause satisfied structurally, since the category hangs off a sense, not a word), broken.* are modes/qualities, friend senses role/relator patterns, with 'underdetermined' a legal value; (b) argument-level: HARD referent sorts stay inside the existing profile-1 3-sort grammar (SomeoneRef/SomethingRef/TimeRef, unchanged — no encoder bump) but are committed per sense and hence well-posed, while FINE selectional typing (break.shatter patient -> material entity; break.violate object -> social/normative object, never material; break.interrupt patient -> process/state) is soft, rank-only, per-sense, under the same forbidden-effects discipline as the g2 import. The pi:011 defect class (sense-specific preference on a word-scoped concept) becomes unmintable because the record's subject IS the sense.
rows.rationale: Separates what the maintainer's requirement governs (identity-bearing per-sense meaning and category) from what stays advisory (selectional preferences), and does both without touching the encoder pin or promoting soft content to hard laws.

rows.claim: Cost anchors for the cheap validation, read from the frozen v2.2 build record: full 84-slot adoption arm worst-case ~ $9.552 (capped $10), Stage-P pilot ~ $1.06, per-judgment ~ $0.008-0.012 on the proxy pair; the sole v2.2 launch attempt died at preflight on pA quota having spent ~$0.12. Therefore the V-A re-judge (~36 slots x 2 judges) and V-B binding-sort measure (~30 slots x 2) each price at ~$1-3 of proxy spend, with the human panel (~1-2 h) as the pre-committed fallback channel if the v2.2 pilot fails its AC1 gate a second time.
rows.rationale: Establishes that judge spend is negligible for Stage A — the binding constraint is explication authoring time, not model dollars — and that the validation inherits an already-priced, already-pre-committed instrument path either way.
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md (worst-case $9.552/$10, pilot $1.06, unit $0.008-0.012); docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md section 0.3 and D (aborted preflight ~$0.12; retirement pre-commitment; human-panel fallback)

rows.claim: Central empirical prediction of the design (inherited from adjudication ASM-1889b, now given a concrete build): re-doing the five g2 sense-channel items (011, 036, 037, 070, 071) with sense-split materials (break.{shatter,violate,malfunction}, make.{create,cause}, find.{locate,discover}, friend.{person,figurative}; ~10-12 sense concepts, ~36 soft-type slots) resolves >=4/5 of the judge disagreements and yields coherent per-sense verdicts (break.shatter range->material entity plausibly judged sound; break.violate range->material unmintable/absent), AND the sense-scoped soundness of the binding referent sorts — the load-bearing quantity ASM-1887 identifies as unmeasured, for which 0.3929 is only a word-scoped lower bound — becomes measurable on the same materials for ~$1-3 additional judge spend. Falsifier: persistent disagreement on sense-split items relocates the defect to the instrument or to the typing content itself — a genuine, non-artifactual correctness signal. Resolution path: the Stage-A build + one re-judge run (proxy pair if the v2.2 pilot passes AC1>=0.65, else human panel). Rider: DESIGN-ONLY; PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; no feasibility conclusion.
rows.rationale: States the design's testable bet separately from its measured grounds so it can never be laundered as a premise; >=4/5 is a pre-stated expectation for reading the outcome, not a pass/fail gate on any thesis.

rows.claim: Scale and cost expectations, each with its resolution path: (a) under the R4 selection rule the full kernel-v1 mints ~110-140 sense concepts from the 54 v0 words (raw same-POS ceiling 434; heavy-tailed — break/make/give/take contribute 4-6 minted senses each, most words 1-2) — resolved by the Stage-C build; (b) stage cost bands: Stage A <= $10 model spend + 2-3 agent-days; Stage B (24 polysemous panel words, ~60-90 senses) ~$25-40 proxy or human-panel hours + 1-2 agent-weeks; Stage C 2-4 agent-weeks + <$50; Stage D absorbed into the scale track's S0 band — resolved by the respective build ledgers; (c) at the 10k+ scale stages the sense multiplier vanishes as a cost category because synset/LU-keyed import delivers sense-split concepts natively (the scale track's existing premise), with the G1 merge census (WN sense distinctions that collapse to identical profile-1 explications) becoming a standing per-stage measured output — resolved by the scale-track S0 build. Rider: DESIGN-ONLY; no feasibility conclusion.
rows.rationale: Separates planning bands from commitments; the authoring bottleneck (not judge dollars) is flagged as the binding resource, with the mitigation (R4 mint-for-need + exclusion lists) named.

rows.claim: Staging + go/no-go ruling (this designer's, confidence 0.8, offered for maintainer approval): GO on Stage A only — sense-split break/make/find/friend and run V-A + V-B — because it is maintainer-required direction regardless of outcome, costs <= $10 + 2-3 agent-days, is decisive in both directions on the adjudication's 5/12 prediction, unblocks the load-bearing sense-scoped binding-soundness measurement, and touches neither the encoder pin nor any frozen record. Stages B (24-word panel subset), C (full kernel-v1, ~110-140 concepts) and D (scale handoff) are GATED on Stage A's readout, not approved here. Sequencing constraints carried from the adjudication verbatim: no further prose iteration or judgment spend on unsplit polysemous concepts; the v2.2 pilot decision (RUN when pA quota returns) is untouched and its outcome only selects V-A/V-B's judgment channel. Honest risk ledger acknowledged in the design: within-frame clustering judgment, profile-1 expressivity merges (G1), inherited instrument risk, authoring as the scarce resource, per-sense typing may still prove unsound (which is the point of measuring), dangling-deprecation leakage. No feasibility conclusion on CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY is stated or implied.
rows.rationale: The ruling row: smallest decisive increment first, everything larger gated on its result, all frozen decisions left standing.
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 6; docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md section D (pilot decision + no-spend-on-unsplit rule)
